title: Classification
description: Session rubrics for Module 2: Classification - All 6 sessions, 15 points each

sessions.title: Introduction to Classification & First Classifier (Iris)
sessions.description: Build and train first classification model using Iris dataset

criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of classification concepts and train/test split

excellent.description: Clearly explains classification vs regression, provides examples, accurately describes train/test split purpose, distinguishes features from labels

good.description: Explains classification and train/test split adequately with minor gaps in explanation or examples

adequate.description: Basic understanding of classification with some confusion about train/test split or feature/label distinction

poor.description: Significant misunderstandings about classification, train/test split, or cannot distinguish features from labels

criteria.description: Successfully implements complete Iris classification pipeline

excellent.description: Code runs without errors, achieves >90% accuracy, includes proper train/test split, well-commented, uses multiple algorithms

good.description: Code runs with minor issues, achieves 80-90% accuracy, proper split, basic comments, at least 2 algorithms

adequate.description: Code runs but below 80% accuracy, or has split issues, minimal comments, only 1 algorithm

poor.description: Code has significant errors, doesn't run, or no proper train/test split implemented

criteria.description: Analyzes results and creates meaningful visualizations

excellent.description: Thorough analysis with insights, includes confusion matrix, feature distributions, algorithm comparison, identifies patterns and draws conclusions

good.description: Adequate analysis with some insights, includes basic visualizations (confusion matrix, distributions), reasonable conclusions

adequate.description: Basic analysis, mostly descriptive, minimal visualizations, limited insights

poor.description: Little to no analysis, missing key visualizations, no meaningful conclusions

sessions.title: Evaluation Metrics & Confusion Matrix
sessions.description: Master evaluation metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1, and confusion matrix interpretation

criteria.description: Demonstrates understanding of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score

excellent.description: Clearly explains all metrics, provides correct formulas, identifies when to use each metric, explains tradeoffs, understands accuracy limitations

good.description: Explains most metrics correctly, understands basic tradeoffs, minor gaps in when to use each

adequate.description: Basic understanding of metrics, some confusion between precision/recall, limited understanding of when to use each

poor.description: Significant confusion about metrics, cannot distinguish precision from recall, doesn't understand accuracy limitations

criteria.description: Correctly interprets and analyzes confusion matrices

excellent.description: Accurately reads confusion matrix, identifies true/false positives/negatives, explains where model gets confused, relates to metrics, provides actionable insights

good.description: Correctly reads confusion matrix, identifies most error types, relates to metrics, basic insights

adequate.description: Basic confusion matrix reading, some errors in identifying TP/FP/TN/FN, limited insights

poor.description: Cannot correctly read confusion matrix, confuses rows/columns, no meaningful analysis

criteria.description: Applies appropriate metrics to real scenarios

excellent.description: Selects appropriate metrics for given scenarios (medical, spam, etc.), justifies choices, understands business impact, considers class imbalance

good.description: Generally selects appropriate metrics, provides basic justification, understands some business context

adequate.description: Sometimes selects appropriate metrics, limited justification, basic business understanding

poor.description: Inappropriate metric selection, no justification, doesn't consider business context

sessions.title: Multiple Classification Algorithms
sessions.description: Compare and contrast Decision Trees, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, KNN, and SVM

excellent.description: Explains how each algorithm makes decisions, identifies strengths/weaknesses, understands hyperparameters, compares approaches conceptually

good.description: Explains most algorithms adequately, identifies some strengths/weaknesses, basic hyperparameter understanding

adequate.description: Basic algorithm understanding, limited ability to compare, minimal hyperparameter knowledge

poor.description: Cannot explain algorithms, no understanding of differences or hyperparameters

criteria.description: Implements and compares multiple algorithms effectively

excellent.description: Implements 4+ algorithms correctly, creates comprehensive comparison (accuracy, speed, interpretability), visualizes results, draws meaningful conclusions

good.description: Implements 3 algorithms correctly, adequate comparison, basic visualization, reasonable conclusions

adequate.description: Implements 2 algorithms, basic comparison (accuracy only), minimal visualization, limited conclusions

poor.description: Implements 1 or no algorithms correctly, no meaningful comparison, missing analysis

criteria.description: Makes informed decisions about which algorithm to use

excellent.description: Selects appropriate algorithm based on multiple factors (accuracy, interpretability, speed, data characteristics), provides strong justification, considers production requirements

good.description: Selects reasonable algorithm, considers 2-3 factors, provides basic justification

adequate.description: Selects algorithm based mainly on accuracy, limited justification, few other factors considered

poor.description: Random or unjustified algorithm selection, no consideration of tradeoffs

sessions.title: Text Classification & Spam Detection
sessions.description: Build spam detector using text preprocessing, vectorization, and classification

criteria.description: Correctly preprocesses text data for ML

excellent.description: Implements complete preprocessing pipeline (lowercase, punctuation removal, tokenization, stop words), understands purpose of each step, handles edge cases

good.description: Implements most preprocessing steps, understands main purposes, basic edge case handling

adequate.description: Implements basic preprocessing, limited understanding of purposes, no edge case handling

poor.description: Minimal or incorrect preprocessing, doesn't understand purposes

criteria.description: Converts text to features and builds effective classifier

excellent.description: Implements both CountVectorizer and TF-IDF, compares approaches, builds spam detector achieving >95% accuracy, tests multiple algorithms, handles imbalanced data

good.description: Implements one vectorization method correctly, achieves 90-95% accuracy, tests 2 algorithms, acknowledges imbalance

adequate.description: Basic vectorization implementation, achieves 80-90% accuracy, single algorithm, ignores imbalance

poor.description: Incorrect vectorization, below 80% accuracy, or code doesn't work

criteria.description: Evaluates spam detector with appropriate metrics and real-world testing

excellent.description: Uses precision/recall/F1 (not just accuracy), creates confusion matrix, tests with new custom emails (10+), analyzes errors, considers false positive vs false negative tradeoffs

good.description: Uses appropriate metrics, confusion matrix, tests with 5+ new emails, basic error analysis

adequate.description: Uses accuracy only, minimal confusion matrix analysis, tests with 1-3 emails, limited error analysis

poor.description: No proper evaluation metrics, no testing with new emails, no error analysis

sessions.title: Customer Churn Prediction & Feature Engineering
sessions.description: Predict customer churn with advanced feature engineering and handling categorical data

criteria.description: Creates meaningful new features from raw data

excellent.description: Creates 5+ engineered features with business logic (tenure categories, avg monthly charge, service combinations), justifies each feature, tests feature importance

good.description: Creates 3-4 engineered features, basic justification, some feature importance analysis

adequate.description: Creates 1-2 engineered features, limited justification, minimal importance analysis

poor.description: No feature engineering or arbitrary features with no justification

excellent.description: Correctly applies Label Encoding and One-Hot Encoding where appropriate, understands differences, handles all categorical variables, applies feature scaling to numerical features

good.description: Correctly encodes most categorical variables, understands main differences, basic scaling

adequate.description: Encodes some variables (may mix up label/one-hot), limited understanding, minimal scaling

poor.description: Incorrect encoding, doesn't handle categorical variables, no scaling

criteria.description: Connects ML results to business insights and actions

excellent.description: Identifies key churn drivers, provides actionable recommendations, considers cost of false positives vs false negatives, analyzes feature importance for business insights, estimates business impact

good.description: Identifies main churn drivers, provides some recommendations, considers metric tradeoffs, basic business insights

adequate.description: Lists churn factors, limited recommendations, minimal business context

poor.description: No business analysis, no recommendations, purely technical focus

sessions.title: Medical Diagnosis, Model Interpretation & Production
sessions.description: Advanced topics including healthcare ML, ROC/AUC, interpretability, and deployment considerations

criteria.description: Understands and applies ROC curves and AUC

excellent.description: Creates ROC curve, calculates AUC, interprets meaning, compares to other metrics, understands threshold tradeoffs, explains significance of AUC value

good.description: Creates ROC curve, calculates AUC, basic interpretation, understands it's better than accuracy for imbalanced data

adequate.description: Creates ROC curve, limited interpretation, basic AUC understanding

poor.description: Cannot create ROC curve or doesn't understand AUC

excellent.description: Analyzes feature importance, explains key decision factors, visualizes decision boundaries (if applicable), relates findings to domain knowledge, considers ethical implications

good.description: Analyzes feature importance, identifies key factors, basic visualizations, some domain connection

adequate.description: Basic feature importance analysis, limited domain connection, minimal visualization

poor.description: No interpretability analysis, black-box approach, no domain consideration

excellent.description: Discusses cross-validation, handles edge cases, considers data drift monitoring, plans for model updates, addresses ethical considerations, documents assumptions and limitations

good.description: Uses cross-validation, considers some edge cases, acknowledges need for monitoring, basic limitations documentation

adequate.description: Basic validation, minimal edge case handling, limited production considerations

poor.description: No production considerations, no validation beyond single train/test, no limitations acknowledged

A.description: Excellent - Exceeds expectations in all areas

B.description: Good - Meets expectations with some areas of excellence

F.description: Failing - Does not meet minimum requirements
